name: Leek and Potato Soup (Marguerite Patten)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the leeks, potatoes, and onion into small pieces. Use a little of the tender green part of the leeks.
2. Melt the margarine in a large saucepan. Add the vegetables and toss in the margarine for 5 minutes.
3. Pour the chicken stock or water into the saucepan. Add a little seasoning.
4. Cover the pan and cook steadily for 25 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender. Do not over-cook, as this will spoil the fresh flavor of the leeks.
5. Sieve or liquidize and reheat. Top with yoghurt or cream.
Variations
Curried Leek and Potato Soup: Blend 2 to 3 teaspoons of curry paste with a little milk, add to the soup above and simmer for a few minutes. If using a small amount of leftover soup, adjust the amount of curry paste.
Watercress and Potato Soup: Use only 2 small leeks and the leaves and tender stalks from a small bunch of watercress. Sieve or liquidize and top with freshly chopped watercress leaves.
Cold Leek and Potato Soup: Add a little milk or single cream to the cold sieved or liquidized soup plus a small quantity of white wine. Chill well.
